seo.page_title: 2020 Audi A6 Avant import from Japan to NZ
seo.meta_description: Import a 2020 Audi A6 Avant wagon from Japan to NZ. Petrol or diesel options, full auction documents and clear landed-cost breakdown.
seo.h1_tag: Import a 2020 Audi A6 Avant to NZ
seo.og_title: 2020 Audi A6 Avant import to New Zealand
seo.og_description: Looking at a 2020 Audi A6 Avant wagon? We help you source petrol or diesel models from Japan with auction documents, clear costs and NZ entry handled step by step.
secondary_keywords: import Audi A6 Avant from Japan

blocks.hero_h1: Import a 2020 Audi A6 Avant to NZ
blocks.hero_subtitle: Looking at a 2020 Audi A6 Avant from Japan? We help you bring in a tidy petrol or diesel wagon with full auction paperwork, clear costs, and NZ entry handled step by step.
proof_points: Transparent pricing — compliance is part of the process
blocks.why_kiwis_buy: The 2020 Audi A6 Avant suits Kiwi buyers who want a roomy European wagon that still works as a reliable daily driver. Petrol and diesel options give you flexibility for open-road trips, while the long boot handles prams, golf clubs or work gear without drama.

savings_block.headline: Where the money goes — from Japan auction to NZ road
savings_block.body: We prepare the costs for the specific car before you bid. The final figure depends on the vehicle price, Japan-side costs, shipping, AWA fee, customs clearance, compliance and on-road costs. Compliance and on-road costs vary by vehicle, so we confirm them individually rather than presenting a generic estimate.

cta_form.headline: Find your Audi A6 Avant — we'll do the searching
cta_form.subtext: Fill in your details to get started. We'll search the Japanese auctions and come back with real options and pricing by email.

blocks.video_caption: Here’s a typical Audi A6 Avant cabin from Japan, with the wide infotainment screens and flat load floor that make it easy to live with day to day.
blocks.main_usp_deep: The 2020 Audi A6 Avant gives you European comfort in a practical wagon body, with both petrol and diesel options available out of Japan. You get a long, usable cargo area that still feels refined on the motorway, and the tech package makes daily driving easier. From our side, we focus on clean auction grades, service history and checking for any electronic issues that could be costly in NZ. That way you typically end up with a good honest car that’s set up for our roads and conditions.

buyer_persona.headline: Is this car right for you?
buyer_persona.body: You might be moving on from a smaller hatch or SUV and want something that still feels upmarket but can handle family life or regular work trips. If you like the idea of a European wagon that’s comfortable on long runs, has space for gear, and you’re happy to keep on top of servicing, the A6 Avant is a solid match.

trust_company.headline: Why import with ImportYourCar.co.nz?
trust_company.body: We’ve been bringing Japanese vehicles into NZ since 2009, with hundreds of wagons and European models in that mix. Our team checks auction sheets, photos and grading so you see the same information we do before any bidding happens. You get a clear cost breakdown from Japan auction price through to landed in NZ, with no surprise add-ons. We’re a real NZ-based crew you can call or email while your Audi A6 Avant makes its way here.

car_specs_benefits.benefit: Gives you a comfortable long-distance car that still has the space of a wagon for family or work gear.
Lets you pick what suits your driving — petrol for mixed city use, diesel for higher mileage and towing.
Easy to load prams, bikes, tools or luggage without needing a big SUV.
Helps with motorway driving, parking and day-to-day comfort on NZ roads.
Typically feels solid on rougher chipseal and holds up well if serviced on time.

cta_mid_page.headline: Keen on an A6 Avant? Let’s run the numbers
cta_mid_page.subtext: Tell us the year, fuel type and budget you have in mind and we’ll price up real cars from the Japanese auctions.
cta_mid_page.button_text: Happy to run the numbers for you.

models_comparison.intro: We compare the exact auction listings available at the time of your search, rather than relying on generic variant assumptions.

rows.notes: We confirm the year, model code, mileage and auction grade from each listing.
We compare only the engine and drivetrain stated on the individual auction sheet.
Photos and the auction sheet are used to compare the equipment, wheels, tyres and any visible modifications.
We translate the available notes and point out repairs, damage or items that need further checking.
Compliance and on-road work are assessed for the selected vehicle; they are not assumed from a generic model guide.

steps.title: Tell us what you're after
steps.body: Fill in the form or message us. We confirm your brief and give you a cost breakdown before anything moves forward.

steps.title: We search the auctions
steps.body: We monitor USS, TAA, HAA and others. For a 2020 Audi A6 Avant, we pay close attention to service records and any notes about electrical or infotainment issues on the auction sheet.

steps.title: You approve, we bid
steps.body: We send you the auction sheet and our recommendation. Once you're happy, you pay a deposit and we bid on your behalf.

steps.title: Shipping to NZ
steps.body: Typically 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. For less common trims or colours, allow a little extra time once the auction clears. We keep you updated along the way.

steps.title: Compliance and delivery
steps.body: The car goes through NZ entry certification and compliance. Once it's through, we arrange delivery or you can collect.

risks_solutions.intro: Importing a 2020 Audi A6 Avant from Japan is straightforward, but it helps to know what to expect.

items.risk: Worried about overpaying or missing hidden mechanical issues?
items.solution: We share recent auction results, estimated landed costs and the full auction sheet, including underbody photos where available. That way you can see condition notes, service stickers and grading before deciding to bid.

items.risk: Hidden costs popping up after you’ve committed
items.solution: We outline the costs for the individual vehicle before you bid: vehicle price, Japan-side costs, shipping, AWA fee, customs clearance, compliance and on-road costs. Variable items are confirmed for the selected car.

items.risk: Not knowing how auction grades translate to real condition
items.solution: We explain the grade, translate key auction comments and point out anything that might need attention in NZ, like tyres, brakes or cosmetic repairs. You get the documentation we see so you can make an informed call.

blocks.how_to_use_iyc: You tell us the Audi A6 Avant you’re after — year, fuel type, budget and any must-have features. We watch the Japanese auctions, shortlist suitable cars and send you the sheets, photos and landed-cost estimates. You only move ahead and pay a deposit once you’ve seen a specific car that works for you.

faq_block.q: Is a 2020 Audi A6 Avant a good daily driver in NZ?
faq_block.a: For most buyers, yes — it’s a comfortable wagon with plenty of space and modern safety tech. As long as you keep on top of servicing and use a workshop that knows European cars, it usually works well as a daily. We can help you target lower-kilometre examples with solid service history.

faq_block.q: How much does it cost to import a 2020 Audi A6 Avant from Japan?
faq_block.a: We price the specific auction vehicle before you bid. The estimate is built from the vehicle price, Japan-side costs, shipping, AWA fee, customs clearance, compliance and on-road costs. Compliance and on-road costs are confirmed individually for the selected car.

faq_block.q: Will a 2020 Audi A6 Avant usually meet NZ compliance standards?
faq_block.a: usually, a 2020 A6 Avant from Japan typically meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no major modifications or structural repairs are noted. Final approval always depends on physical inspection at entry certification in NZ.

faq_block.q: How long does it take to get my Audi A6 Avant after we win an auction?
faq_block.a: From auction win to the car being ready in NZ, you’re typically looking at around 6–10 weeks. That includes export prep in Japan, shipping, port processing, entry certification, compliance work and on-road steps.

faq_block.q: What auction grade should I aim for on a 2020 A6 Avant?
faq_block.a: Most buyers look for grade 4 or better with a tidy interior grade, but sometimes a lower grade with honest cosmetic issues can offer value. We explain the grade, translate key comments and highlight anything that might need mechanical or cosmetic attention once it’s in NZ.

footer_cta.headline: Ready to price up a 2020 Audi A6 Avant?
footer_cta.subtext: Tell us what you’re after and we’ll come back with real Japanese auction options, full documentation and a clear landed-cost estimate.
footer_cta.button_text: Happy to run the numbers for you.

subscription_block.headline: Stay in the loop
subscription_block.subtext: Get updates on fresh Audi A6 Avant listings in Japan, plus plain-English guides on importing to NZ.

blocks.company_positioning: ImportYourCar.co.nz is an NZ-based team that’s been importing vehicles from Japan since 2009. We focus on matching buyers with the right cars, sharing full auction documentation and clear landed-cost breakdowns. Most of our work is with everyday Kiwis wanting a good honest car that’s cheap to run.

landed_cost_breakdown.intro: We prepare the landed-cost estimate for the selected vehicle before you bid.

rows.notes: The agreed purchase price for the specific vehicle.
The relevant costs in Japan for that vehicle and purchase.
Freight for the confirmed vehicle and sailing.
The applicable AWA fee for the import.
Clearance costs for the vehicle on arrival.
Confirmed after the vehicle and its requirements are known.
Confirmed individually for the vehicle before you proceed.

auction_checklist.check: Check full service history and service stickers
auction_checklist.why: Regular oil changes and scheduled maintenance are important on European engines and help avoid expensive repairs later.

auction_checklist.check: Inspect auction sheet for electrical or infotainment notes
auction_checklist.why: The A6 Avant has complex electronics, so any mention of warning lights or screen issues needs to be understood before bidding.

auction_checklist.check: Look closely at underbody and corrosion photos
auction_checklist.why: Helps pick cars that have been kept away from heavy snow or coastal exposure, which is better for long-term ownership in NZ.

auction_checklist.check: Review panel and paint notes for previous repairs
auction_checklist.why: Tells you whether the car has had cosmetic or structural work, which can affect both value and NZ entry certification.

auction_checklist.check: Confirm wheel, suspension and exhaust are close to factory
auction_checklist.why: Heavily modified cars can face extra checks at entry certification and may need work to meet NZ standards.

not_for_you_block.headline: Who this may not suit
items: You want the cheapest possible wagon to run and aren’t keen on European servicing costs.
You prefer a high-riding SUV and don’t want a lower wagon body.
You’re unlikely to keep up with regular servicing, which is important on a 2020 Audi A6 Avant.
You want a car you can use hard off-road; this is more of a sealed-road and light gravel car.

alternatives_block.intro: If you’re not sure the 2020 Audi A6 Avant is the right fit, these alternatives may be worth a look.

rows.better_if: You want sharper, sportier handling and don’t mind a bit less boot space.
rows.tradeoff: You typically give up some cargo room and may find the ride firmer on rougher NZ roads.

rows.better_if: You’re chasing more comfort features and a softer ride feel.
rows.tradeoff: Usually costs more to buy at auction and can be pricier to maintain.

rows.better_if: You value safety tech and a relaxed driving feel over badge prestige.
rows.tradeoff: Brand image is a bit more low-key and some parts may need to be ordered in when servicing.

engine_drivetrain_block.headline: Engine and drivetrain checks for the A6 Avant
engine_drivetrain_block.body: We assess the exact vehicle offered at auction. The listing, auction sheet, photos and available records tell us what we can verify before a bid; they do not replace a physical inspection.
checklist: Confirm the exact engine and drivetrain shown in the auction listing.
Review the available photos and condition notes for visible issues or modifications.
Check the auction sheet and seller notes for reported mechanical or warning-light comments.
Review service history and mileage records where supplied.
Explain what is confirmed, what is unknown and what should be allowed for after arrival.
